design_philosophy.core_loop: Advance through side-scrolling stages, defeat waves of enemies and bosses using character-specific moves, weapons, and command-based special attacks powered by a Super Move gauge
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing aggressive offense (to fill Super gauge) against defensive positioning and crowd control; managing limited health between food pickups
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a martial artist or street fighter using signature weapons and techniques to clean Metro City of organized crime; experiencing fluid, input-reactive combat reminiscent of Street Fighter
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Integrates fighting game mechanics (command inputs, Super gauge, character specialization) into beat 'em up framework; emphasizes player agency through branching stage paths, multiple characters with distinct move sets, and dynamic difficulty-based endings—positioning itself as narrative-forward and mechanically deeper than its predecessor, despite critical reception suggesting incremental evolution

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives-based system with health restoration via food pickups
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system available (accessible via debug menu in all versions)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression with no mid-stage checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple difficulty settings available; difficulty selection affects final ending and narrative path; branching stage design provides optional route variation that may alter enemy/boss encounters

progression.upgrade_system: No persistent upgrades; Super Move gauge fills during combat and depletes on activation; character-specific weapon specialization grants unique combo attacks when equipped
progression.unlock_mechanism: Character selection (4 playable characters available from start: Guy, Haggar, Lucia, Dean); stage paths branch based on player navigation choices
replayability_hooks: Four distinct playable characters with unique move sets and abilities
Branching stage paths that alter enemy/boss encounters
Multiple endings determined by character choice, path taken, and difficulty setting
CPU partner mode for single-player co-op variation

world_building_techniques: Plot-driven stage progression through Metro City locations
Character introductions via plot setup (Lucia as detective, Dean as vengeance-driven fighter)
Contextual enemy types reflecting stage setting (sewer enemies, urban gang members)
Multiple endings narrative variation based on player choices and difficulty

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters function as thematic stage conclusions; difficulty tuning reflects character selection and stage branching; some bosses appear in multiple locations due to branching mechanics, allowing varied approach contexts
notable_bosses: Callman (Stage 2 boss, encountered in two possible locations due to branching)

faction_indicators: Metro City Police Special Crimes Unit (represented by Lucia)
Street fighter community (represented by Dean's character arc)
danger_signals: Downtown riots initiated by Skull Cross Gang
Family murder motivation for Dean character
Criminal organization hierarchy implied through boss encounters
environmental_storytelling: Stage-specific locations (downtown, sewer, urban areas) reflect gang territorial control
Breakable environment objects (drums containing weapons/items) suggest looted or contested spaces
Enemy variety per stage indicates localized gang operations
